findings.description: Duplicate import of contextmanager on line 12 and 13. Line 13 is redundant and will be ignored by Python. No functional impact but indicates a copy-paste error.
findings.evidence: from contextlib import contextmanager  # line 12; from contextlib import contextmanager  # line 13 (duplicate)

findings.description: Unreachable code after early return. Line 1124 is a return statement that exits the function, but lines 1125-1220 below it are syntactically valid Python and will never execute. This is a logic error where the return was placed before code that should have run.
findings.evidence: Line 1124: return {vid: (False, None, 'Notebook failed') for vid in video_ids}; Line 1125: add_sources_elapsed_s = 0.0  # UNREACHABLE

findings.description: Use of 'retire_elapsed_s if retire_elapsed_s in locals() else 0.0' pattern in _ensure_notebook metrics. Since retire_elapsed_s is assigned inside an except block at line 1051 via a bare except clause, it may not exist in locals() even on the success path.
findings.evidence: Line 1064: retire_elapsed_s: retire_elapsed_s if 'retire_elapsed_s' in locals() else 0.0

findings.description: Writer thread joins with 5s timeout but _stopped flag is set BEFORE queue drain. When _stop() is called, it sets _stopped=True immediately, then calls join(). The writer loop checks _stopped after reading one item from the queue, meaning if an item is queued and the writer is between queue.get() and the next iteration, the join() will wait the full 5s even though the queue still has items.
findings.evidence: _stopped flag set at line 70 before join() at line 75. Writer loop at line 124 checks _stopped after queue.get().

findings.description: Writer thread uses unbounded queue.get() which blocks indefinitely. If the queue is empty and the thread has nothing to do, it blocks forever on queue.get(). The thread should use get(timeout=...) or handle the None shutdown signal differently.
findings.evidence: Line 121: while True: item = self._write_queue.get()  # blocks forever if queue empty; if item is None: break

findings.description: TOCTOU in _record_source_429: source argument is a method token (e.g. 'whisper') but record_429 expects channel_url as primary key. Code attempts to resolve via _get_source_for_video(video_id) but this may return None if video has no source, causing cross-terminal sync to silently fail.
findings.evidence: Line 530: channel_url = _get_source_for_video(video_id); Line 532: _get_scheduler().record_429(channel_url)  # silently skipped if channel_url is None

findings.description: TOCTOU in yield_next: between checking if a channel is in cooldown (_is_in_cooldown) and marking a video as attempting (_record_attempting), another terminal can simultaneously yield the same video. The function yields the video_id before the write to download_archive is committed.
findings.evidence: Line 232: if self._is_in_cooldown(channel): continue  # time-of-check; Line 268: self._record_attempting(video_id, channel)  # time-of-use - non-atomic with yield

findings.description: EXCLUSIVE transaction in _record_attempting prevents inter-process races but does NOT prevent same-process multiple threads from racing. BEGIN EXCLUSIVE only blocks other processes. Two threads in same process can simultaneously pass re-check and both insert same video_id as attempting.
findings.evidence: Line 121: conn.execute('BEGIN EXCLUSIVE'); Line 123-129: re-check inside transaction; only inter-process blocking guaranteed.

findings.description: _ensure_nlm_auth swallows all exceptions in the login retry path. If subprocess.run raises an exception (not just a failed return code), it is silently caught by the bare except Exception: pass block, and the function proceeds to call login anyway. This means transient errors during the --check probe are silently ignored.
findings.evidence: Line 1351: except Exception: pass  # silently swallows all exceptions including FileNotFoundError

summary: Found 9 issues: 4 bugs/logic errors, 3 race conditions, 2 TOCTOU vulnerabilities, 1 error-handling gap. Highest severity: P1 unreachable code in nlm_batch.py line 1124 and P2 race conditions in batch_scheduler._record_attempting and retry_queue writer shutdown.
